body.html:
Robbert-Jan,

Most security leads tell us the same thing:

“The training itself is valuable. The setup and reliability is what makes it stressful.”

That’s the exact problem we remove.

Before I stop reaching out, do you want access to:
1) the free trial
2) the CTF Planning & Execution Guide

Or should I close the loop?

Reply “Access” if you’re interested; otherwise, I’ll close the loop.

Sincerely,
Hannah

items.subject: Quick question about security training at Nike

body.html:
Hi Jiggyasu,

I was looking at Nike and your role in security.

Quick question, do you ever run (or plan to run) hands-on security training for your team?
Things like internal labs, workshops, or CTF-style exercises.

When teams use CTFs for training, the content is usually solid, but the prep around infra, setup, testing, and stability tends to eat a lot of time.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that layer, so training sessions don’t turn into infrastructure projects.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to offer two free resources we give security teams:

1) A free trial — spin up a full CTF environment with zero DevOps
2) A practical CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
– pre-training setup
– smooth execution during the session
– post-training wrap-up
– a checklist to avoid downtime in front of trainees

Happy to send both if this is ever relevant for you. Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Hannah

Muthu, quick follow-up.

Not sure if hands-on security training or CTF-style exercises are something you deal with right now.

If they are, I can share:
• free trial access
• the CTF Planning & Execution Guide

If not, no worries at all. Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Hannah

items.subject: Quick question about security training at NTT DATA, Inc.

body.html:
Hi Harvey,

I was looking at NTT DATA, Inc. and your role in security.

Quick question, do you ever run (or plan to run) hands-on security training for your team?
Things like internal labs, workshops, or CTF-style exercises.

When teams use CTFs for training, the content is usually solid, but the prep around infra, setup, testing, and stability tends to eat a lot of time.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that layer, so training sessions don’t turn into infrastructure projects.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to offer two free resources we give security teams:

1) A free trial — spin up a full CTF environment with zero DevOps
2) A practical CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
– pre-training setup
– smooth execution during the session
– post-training wrap-up
– a checklist to avoid downtime in front of trainees

Happy to send both if this is ever relevant for you. Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Hannah

Annys,

Most security leads tell us the same thing:

“The training itself is valuable. The setup and reliability is what makes it stressful.”

That’s the exact problem we remove.

Before I stop reaching out, do you want access to:
1) the free trial

Or should I close the loop?

Reply “Access” if you’re interested; otherwise, I’ll close the loop.

items.subject: Most security leads tell us this

body.html:
Jon,

Most security leads tell us the same thing:

“The training itself is valuable. The setup and reliability is what makes it stressful.”

That’s the exact problem we remove.

Before I stop reaching out, do you want access to:
1) the free trial
2) the CTF Planning & Execution Guide

Or should I close the loop?

Reply “Access” if you’re interested; otherwise, I’ll close the loop.

Sincerely,
Hannah

items.subject: Quick question about security training at Cisco

body.html:
Hi Richard,

I was looking at Cisco and your role in security.

Quick question, do you ever run (or plan to run) hands-on security training for your team?
Things like internal labs, workshops, or CTF-style exercises.

When teams use CTFs for training, the content is usually solid, but the prep around infra, setup, testing, and stability tends to eat a lot of time.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that layer, so training sessions don’t turn into infrastructure projects.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to offer two free resources we give security teams:

1) A free trial — spin up a full CTF environment with zero DevOps
2) A practical CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
– pre-training setup
– smooth execution during the session
– post-training wrap-up
– a checklist to avoid downtime in front of trainees

Happy to send both if this is ever relevant for you. Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Hannah

items.subject: Quick question about security training at Cisco

body.html:
Hi Sohail,

I was looking at Cisco and your role in security.

Quick question, do you ever run (or plan to run) hands-on security training for your team?
Things like internal labs, workshops, or CTF-style exercises.

When teams use CTFs for training, the content is usually solid, but the prep around infra, setup, testing, and stability tends to eat a lot of time.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that layer, so training sessions don’t turn into infrastructure projects.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to offer two free resources we give security teams:

1) A free trial — spin up a full CTF environment with zero DevOps
2) A practical CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
– pre-training setup
– smooth execution during the session
– post-training wrap-up
– a checklist to avoid downtime in front of trainees

Happy to send both if this is ever relevant for you. Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Hannah

When teams use CTFs for training, the content is usually solid, but the prep around infra, setup, testing, and stability tends to eat a lot of time.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that layer, so training sessions don’t turn into infrastructure projects.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to offer two free resources we give security teams:

1) A free trial — spin up a full CTF environment with zero DevOps
2) A practical CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
– pre-training setup
– smooth execution during the session
– post-training wrap-up
– a checklist to avoid downtime in front of trainees

Happy to send both if this is ever relevant for you. Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

items.subject: Automatic reply: Keypath Education

body.text:
Hello,

I will be out of the office returning February 10th. For immediate assistance please contact Floyd Mack. He can be reached directly at 570-821-0309 or by email at Floyd.Mack@onlinenursingdegrees.wilkes.edu.

I look forward to connecting with you upon my return.

Thank you,

Notice: This email, including any attachments, may contain confidential, privileged and/or proprietary information, and is intended for the named recipient(s) only. If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that disclosing, copying, distributing or taking any action in reliance on the content of this email is strictly prohibited. Please contact the sender by reply email if you received this email in error, and destroy all copies of the original message.

Hi Dipen,

I was looking at CGI and your role in security.

Quick question, do you ever run (or plan to run) hands-on security training for your team?
Things like internal labs, workshops, or CTF-style exercises.

When teams use CTFs for training, the content is usually solid, but the prep around infra, setup, testing, and stability tends to eat a lot of time.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that layer, so training sessions don’t turn into infrastructure projects.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to offer two free resources we give security teams:

1) A free trial — spin up a full CTF environment with zero DevOps
2) A practical CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
– pre-training setup
– smooth execution during the session
– post-training wrap-up
– a checklist to avoid downtime in front of trainees

Happy to send both if this is ever relevant for you. Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Hannah

When teams use CTFs for training, the content is usually solid, but the prep around infra, setup, testing, and stability tends to eat a lot of time.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that layer, so training sessions don’t turn into infrastructure projects.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to offer two free resources we give security teams:

1) A free trial — spin up a full CTF environment with zero DevOps
2) A practical CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
– pre-training setup
– smooth execution during the session
– post-training wrap-up
– a checklist to avoid downtime in front of trainees

Happy to send both if this is ever relevant for you. Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Hannah

Hi Craig,

I was looking at Cole Engineering Services, Inc. (CESI), a By Light Company and noticed you’re organizing CTFs regularly.

Quick question, how much time do you usually lose *before* the CTF even starts?
Infra setup, DevOps, testing, last-minute fixes, etc.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that entire layer.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to share two free resources we give organizers:

1) A free trial (spin up a full CTF environment without DevOps)
2) A short CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
  • Pre-CTF Planning
  • CTF Day Execution
  • Post-CTF Wrap-Up
  • Planning Checklist

Happy to send both if useful.

Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Noah

“Running the CTF is the easy part. Preparing it is what hurts.”

That’s exactly what we remove.

Before I stop reaching out, do you want access to:
1) the free trial
2) the CTF Planning & Execution Guide
Or should I close the loop?

Reply “Access” if you’re interested; otherwise, I’ll close the loop.

Sincerely,

items.subject: Automatic reply: Most organizers tell us this

body.text:
Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren,

ich bin am Montag und Dienstag, den 9. und 10. Februar nicht im Büro. Während meiner Abwesenheit werden meine E-Mails nicht weitergeleitet.

Ab dem 11. Februar 2026 stehe ich Ihnen gerne wieder persönlich zur Verfügung.

In dringenden Fällen kontaktieren Sie bitte meinen Kollegen Herrn Elmir Brkc elmir.brkc@phished.io .

Mit freundlichen Grüßen
Juste Simelyte
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hello,

I will be out of the office on the 9th and 10th of February. During my absence, my emails will not be forwarded.

From February 11th, 2026, I will be happy to assist you personally again.

In urgent cases, please contact my colleague Mr. Elmir Brkc at elmir.brkc@phished.io.

Best regards,
Juste Simelyte

As promised, here’s the CTF Planning & Execution Guide:
👉 https://start.simulationslabs.com/assets/ctf-planning-execution-guide.pdf

It covers:
– pre-event setup & infra checklist
– common failure points teams run into
– smooth execution on event day
– post-CTF wrap-up and evaluation

Hope it’s useful. 

If you have a CTF coming up and want to simplify setup and execution, happy to share how teams usually use our platform.

Best,
Hannah

On Wed, Feb 4, 2026 at 10:03 AM Hannah Adam <hannah@simlabs-team.com> wrote:
Hi Daniel,

I was looking at GT Edge AI and noticed you’re organizing CTFs regularly.

Quick question, how much time do you usually lose *before* the CTF even starts?
Infra setup, DevOps, testing, last-minute fixes,etc.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that entire layer.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to share two free resources we give organizers:

1) A free trial (spin up a full CTF environment without DevOps)
2) A short CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
  • Pre-CTF Planning
  • CTF Day Execution
  • Post-CTF Wrap-Up
  • Planning Checklist

Happy to send both if useful.

Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Noah

items.subject: Automatic reply: Cole Engineering Services, Inc. (CESI), a By Light Company

body.text:
I am currently out-of-office for the week on military leave, and I will return on Monday (3/9). For urgent matters, please call or text my cell at 412-215-2809.

For non-urgent matters, please contact Jennifer Headden - Jennifer.Headden@cesicorp.com.

Regards,

Sean Smith
Range Operations Lead
Persistent Cyber Training Enterprise – Operations Group
Cole Engineering Services, Inc. (CESI)
E: sean.smith@cesicorp.com<mailto:sean.smith@cesicorp.com> | sean.t.smith2.ctr@mail.mil<mailto:sean.t.smith2.ctr@mail.mil>
C: 412-215-2809

Hi Lenny,

I was looking at Mohegan Sun and noticed you’re organizing CTFs regularly.

Quick question, how much time do you usually lose *before* the CTF even starts?
Infra setup, DevOps, testing, last-minute fixes, etc.

We built a CTF hosting platform specifically to remove that entire layer.

Instead of pitching anything, I wanted to share two free resources we give organizers:

1) A free trial (spin up a full CTF environment without DevOps)
2) A short CTF Planning & Execution Guide covering:
  • Pre-CTF Planning
  • CTF Day Execution
  • Post-CTF Wrap-Up
  • Planning Checklist

Happy to send both if useful.

Reply “Send” and I’ll send them over.

Sincerely,
Noah
